title: Astracantha pennata
taxonomy: Astracantha pennata is a species of thorny shrub in the legume family, Fabaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Western Asia. The plant belongs to the genus Astracantha, which was previously known as Oxytropis. Astracantha pennata is a member of the subfamily Faboideae, which includes many other legume species. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships within the Fabaceae family.
uses: Astracantha pennata, also known as the silver cluster pea, has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
